purpose: The authoritative data-protocol reference for every file the master agent writes to a dashboard. Defines exact schemas, write timings, write rules, and exceptions for initialization.json, logs.json, master_state.json, and metrics.json. Includes the archive-before-clear NON-NEGOTIABLE protocol. This is the single source of truth for master file-write semantics; all other master docs reference it.
gotchas: ALL dashboard writes MUST use absolute paths starting with {tracker_root} — relative 'dashboards/' paths resolve to {project_root} when CWD is the project, creating dashboard files inside the target project. The PreToolUse hook enforce-tracker-root-writes.sh blocks this.
initialization.json is WRITE-ONCE with exactly THREE exceptions: repair task creation, circuit breaker replanning, and !add_task injection. Any other update is a violation.
Atomic full-file writes are MANDATORY — partial JSON silently stops all dashboard updates until corrected. Always read -> parse -> modify in memory -> stringify (2-space indent) -> write whole file.
Do NOT modify or remove the '_instructions' key in data files — it is metadata for the master agent and does not affect rendering.
The 'ide' dashboard is permanent and reserved — master agents must NEVER overwrite ide/initialization.json with swarm plan data.
task.created is IMMUTABLE — never overwrite once set. If you regenerate it on every write you destroy historical ordering and elapsed-time computation.

Removed fields (started_at, completed_at, overall_status, completed_tasks, failed_tasks on task; status, assigned_agent, started_at, completed_at, summary on agents; status, completed on waves) are ALL derived by the dashboard from progress files — do NOT write them.
Server re-reads the full file on every change (~100ms broadcast latency) — invalid JSON freezes the dashboard at last valid state.
Repair task ID format: '{failed_wave}.{next_index}r' — the 'r' suffix is what marks it as a repair task and what the failure-recovery system uses to detect double failures.
Use 'deviation' log level (yellow) for plan divergences — not 'warn' — or filtering breaks.
patterns: Master writes exactly four files during a swarm: initialization.json, logs.json, master_state.json, metrics.json. Plus plan.json which must exist FIRST (hook-enforced via validate-plan-required.sh).
logs.json append-only: read full file -> parse -> append entry -> stringify -> write full file.

Repair task append: new agents[] entry with id '{wave}.{next}r', title 'REPAIR: {original}', same wave/layer/directory, identical depends_on; increment task.total_tasks and waves[].total; rewire all references in other agents' depends_on arrays.
Archive-before-clear: read initialization.json -> if task!=null copy entire dashboard dir to Archive/{YYYY-MM-DD}_{task_name}/ -> then delete progress files and reset initialization.json/logs.json.
Dashboard merges initialization.json (static) with progress/{task_id}.json files (dynamic) client-side — all stat counts derive from progress files.
conventions: task object fields: name (kebab-case slug), type ('Waves'|'Chains'), directory, prompt, project, project_root (absolute), created (ISO 8601 immutable), total_tasks, total_waves.
agents[] fields: id ('{wave}.{index}' or '{wave}.{index}r'), title (~40 char verb phrase), wave (matches waves[].id), layer (optional category), directory (optional), depends_on (string[]).
waves[] fields: id, name (descriptive — UI prepends 'Wave {id}: '), total (count of agents in wave).
chains[] required when task.type='Chains': id, name, tasks[] (ordered agent IDs, each agent in exactly one chain).

logs.json entry fields: timestamp (ISO 8601), task_id ('0.0' for orchestrator else '{wave}.{idx}'), agent ('Orchestrator' or 'Agent N'), level (info|warn|error|debug|permission|deviation), message, task_name.
Permission popup trigger: write log entry with level='permission' BEFORE asking in terminal.
